author: straightUp
head: Mar 17, 2026 03:08:10
quote: Oops. A Republic and a Democracy are 2 DIFFERENT types of government.  Look it up and read it.  How many times does the word Democracy show up in the Constitution?  Go ahead and guess…..
post:
Seriously? You think just because the word "democracy" doesn't show up in the Constitution that means a republic can't BE a democracy? So, if I get a ticket for parking my vehicle in a loading zone, are you saying I can just toss it? After all, I'm driving a truck and it doesn't say anywhere on my ticket ANYTHING about a truck. Clearly the laOperaw is referring to vehicles, not trucks. And here's where your argument closes the case... because the world "vehicle" is in the law and "truck" is not, THAT means that vehicles and trucks are two DIFFERENT types of transportation.

Maybe you need to start with something simple like, oh I dunno... a dictionary.

A republic is ANY form of government that ruled not my a monarchy but by an elected president. That's it. Any dictionary will tell you that in so many words. Anything more is an elaboration on specific implementations.

And guess what an election is? It's a feature of democracy. Any country that has a president is a republic and any republic is governed by some form of democracy or it wouldn't be a republic.
